institutional_unit: Optum as manufacturer of the Impact Pro population-health analytics product
decision_process: Design, validation, and revision of the proxy target used to rank patients for care-management attention
sampling_rule: Retrospective assessment of the peer-reviewed study and contemporaneous regulatory identification available at the publication cutoff.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342
New York State DFS and DOH joint letter to UnitedHealth Group, 25 Oct. 2019: https://www.dfs.ny.gov/reports-and-publications/comment-letters/dfs-doh-joint-letter-uhgi-20191025

requested_missing_artifacts: Design-stage evidence register supporting selection of healthcare cost as the target
substantive_findings.rationale: The sources explain the proxy failure but do not disclose the manufacturer's contemporaneous evidence-access record when the target was selected.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Internal deliberation, alternatives, dissent, or review of proxy validity before publication
substantive_findings.rationale: No public artifact reconstructs the manufacturer's pre-publication judgment about whether cost adequately represented health need.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342
New York State DFS and DOH joint letter to UnitedHealth Group, 25 Oct. 2019: https://www.dfs.ny.gov/reports-and-publications/comment-letters/dfs-doh-joint-letter-uhgi-20191025

requested_missing_artifacts: Authority records showing who could reject the proxy choice or stop deployment
substantive_findings.rationale: The public record does not identify an operative internal authority that could alter the target before the external finding.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Customer or patient challenge channel and resulting reversal records
substantive_findings.rationale: No manufacturer-level contestability record is public for individual scores or deployment consequences.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342

requested_missing_artifacts: Records identifying and remediating patients affected under the original proxy
substantive_findings.rationale: The documented technical revision concerns future scoring; the public record does not establish remediation for prior allocation effects.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Obermeyer et al., Science 366 (2019) 447-453, doi:10.1126/science.aax2342: https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342
New York State DFS and DOH joint letter to UnitedHealth Group, 25 Oct. 2019: https://www.dfs.ny.gov/reports-and-publications/comment-letters/dfs-doh-joint-letter-uhgi-20191025

substantive_findings.rationale: The published record documents a material redesign of the prediction target and a substantial reduction in measured bias after the problem was identified.
substantive_findings.ambiguity_note: The reform appears externally prompted; the current schema records the exercised change but not whether it was institution-initiated.

telemetry_integrity.provenance: The study and regulatory letter are traceable public artifacts.
telemetry_integrity.completeness: The public record documents the external finding and technical response but not the full internal design and governance trail.
telemetry_integrity.edit_authority: Internal record ownership and edit permissions are not public.
telemetry_integrity.independence: The strongest evidence was produced by external researchers and regulators rather than the manufacturer's own audit system.
telemetry_integrity.tamper_evidence: No public tamper-evidence controls are documented for internal design records.
telemetry_integrity.retention: No public retention information is available for the relevant internal records.
telemetry_integrity.rationale: External evidence supports one strong reform finding but cannot establish integrity across the manufacturer's broader governance record.

summary: The manufacturer has one documented substantive Reform finding; every other dimension remains insufficiently evidenced in the public record.
limitations: The assessment separates the manufacturer from deploying health systems to avoid attributing one actor's reform to the entire decision chain.
The period fields represent the public evidence cutoff rather than the full commercial lifespan of the product.
The assessment does not determine legal compliance or the adequacy of remedies for previously affected patients.
